name: Roast Stuffed Shoulder of Lamb
recipeInstructions:
1. Open the meat and place stuffing near the center. Roll up firmly with string at one-inch intervals.
2. Place the meat on a rack in the roasting tin. Dust with flour (this helps crisp the fat) and sprinkle with rosemary. Place a skinned, crushed clove of garlic beneath the rack.
3. Cook in a very moderate oven (350°F, Gas Mark 3) for 45 minutes per pound of stuffed weight.
4. Remove roast to a heated platter and keep it warm while making gravy in the roasting tin.
5. Pour off all but one tablespoonful of fat. Stir in one tablespoon flour. Gradually add about three-quarters pint hot stock (e.g., liquid in which vegetables have been cooked) and boil up.
6. Adjust seasoning to taste, strain, and serve. Usual accompaniments are mint sauce or redcurrant jelly, roast or creamed potatoes, carrots, cauliflower, or peas.
